title: Relevance Realization
abstract: This course develops Vervaeke's central technical construct: relevance realization, the non-propositional, embodied process by which the mind-body system dynamically selects what matters out of a combinatorially explosive sea of available information. Students will be able to explain why relevance cannot be explained by representations or a central executive, articulate the key constraints any adequate theory must satisfy (non-circularity, auto-poetic grounding, scale-invariant self-organization), and connect relevance realization to meaning, agency, and the four kinds of knowing. In the series arc, relevance realization serves as Vervaeke's naturalistic foundation for consciousness, wisdom, religio, and the possibility of responding to the meaning crisis — making it one of the most load-bearing concepts in the entire lecture series.

modules.title: Why Relevance Cannot Be Computed: The Combinatorial Explosion
modules.learningObjective: Explain why the space of relevant information is combinatorially explosive and why this makes relevance realization impossible to achieve by exhaustive search or explicit rule.

expositionPassages.phrase: you have to ignore the information, not even check it … See Relevance Realization isn't the application of a definition.
expositionPassages.note: Vervaeke's key point that RR precedes and enables any definition-checking

socraticSeeds.prompt: When you decide what is relevant to solving a problem, do you check every piece of information you have ever encountered and then filter it? If not, how does the relevant subset come to your attention?

Vervaeke argues that applying a definition of relevance cannot itself determine what is relevant, because you first have to decide which parts of the definition apply. Does that seem like an infinite regress? What would stop the regress?
Consider a chess grandmaster who 'sees' the right move while a computer brute-forces millions of positions. What does this difference suggest about how human minds handle the combinatorial explosion?
If you cannot explain relevance by invoking a rule or a homunculus that 'knows' what matters, where could relevance come from? What kinds of processes might produce it without presupposing it?

misconceptionBranches.misconception: Relevance realization is a conscious process of filtering information — we decide what is relevant.
misconceptionBranches.correction: Relevance realization is sub-representational and pre-categorical; it is the process that makes conscious cognition possible, not a product of it.

misconceptionBranches.misconception: A sufficiently powerful computer could, in principle, solve the relevance problem by checking everything.
misconceptionBranches.correction: The combinatorial explosion makes exhaustive search not merely impractical but in principle impossible for any real-time agent — the relevant subset must emerge through self-organization, not search.

checkForUnderstanding.prompt: Why does Vervaeke say that relevance realization cannot be explained by representations, computations, or a central executive — and what does this imply about where we should look for its mechanism?

modules.title: Aspectuality and Salience Tagging: How Relevance Is Structured
modules.learningObjective: Explain what aspectuality is and how enactive demonstrative reference (salience tagging) underlies all categorical cognition.

expositionPassages.phrase: a set of features that are relevant to each other and then a set of features that have been structurally functionally organised so that they have co-relevance, is then relevant to me. That's what an aspect is
I need demonstrative reference I need enactive demonstrative reference before I can do any categorisation. Look, if I'm going to categorize things I need to mentally group them together.
expositionPassages.note: Pre-categorical salience tagging is the condition for all categorization

socraticSeeds.prompt: When you look at a face and immediately see it as sad, are you first seeing a collection of features (downturned mouth, furrowed brow) and then inferring sadness, or is the sadness somehow immediate? What does your answer suggest about how relevance is structured?

Vervaeke says that before you can categorize something you must first 'point at' it — mark it as a here-now focus. Can you have a concept without first being able to enactively single out instances? What would concepts be without that?

An aspect is described as a set of features organized so they have co-relevance. How does this explain why changing one feature of a situation (e.g., finding out a smile was sarcastic) can reorganize how all the other features seem?
If relevance is always perspectival — always relative to an agent — does that mean it is purely subjective? Or is there a sense in which the relevance is real?

misconceptionBranches.misconception: Relevance is a property of objects in the world, independent of any agent.
misconceptionBranches.correction: Relevance is transjective — it emerges in the co-created relationship between organism and environment, neither purely 'in' the world nor purely projected by the mind.

misconceptionBranches.misconception: Aspectuality means we see representations of aspects, not aspects themselves.
misconceptionBranches.correction: Representations presuppose aspects — they foreground a subset of features as relevant but do not generate relevance from scratch. Aspectuality is a constraint on representations, not something produced by them.

checkForUnderstanding.prompt: Why does Vervaeke say that any theory of relevance realization must explain relevance without presupposing relevance in its explanatory machinery — and how does enactive demonstrative reference attempt to satisfy that constraint?

modules.title: Auto-poetic Grounding and Scale-Invariant Self-Organization
modules.learningObjective: Explain why relevance realization must be grounded in constitutive goals internal to a self-organizing system, and why it must operate simultaneously at multiple levels.

expositionPassages.phrase: The goals that are the originating source of Relevance Realisation have to be internal to the Relevance Realisation process.
expositionPassages.note: Auto-poetic grounding: goals cannot be externally represented targets

expositionPassages.phrase: Relevance Realisation has to be happening at multiple levels of cognition in a simultaneous, self-organizing fashion. That's why it can lead to insight.
expositionPassages.note: Multi-level self-organization as both constraint and explanation for insight

expositionPassages.phrase: relevance realization is inherently interested in itself because, as I've argued, that's constitutive of it being a self-organizing, self-correcting, self-optimizing, self-developing, evolving process
expositionPassages.note: RR's reflexive self-interest as central to its nature

socraticSeeds.prompt: If a thermostat 'wants' to maintain 70 degrees only because a human set it, does it have genuine goals? What would it take for a system to have goals that are genuinely internal to it — not assigned from outside?

Relevance realization happens at multiple levels simultaneously — from individual features up to holistic gestalt. Why might this multi-level, simultaneous operation be necessary for insight? Could insight happen if only one level were active at a time?

Vervaeke says relevance realization is 'inherently interested in itself.' What does it mean for a cognitive process to be self-interested — and why is this reflexivity constitutive of wisdom rather than narcissism?

If the goals driving relevance realization must be internal rather than externally imposed, what does this imply about whether a purely designed AI system could ever genuinely realize relevance in the same way a living organism does?

misconceptionBranches.misconception: Relevance realization is a single mechanism located in one brain region, like a relevance-filter.
misconceptionBranches.correction: Relevance realization operates self-organizingly at multiple levels simultaneously — it is a distributed, scale-invariant process with no single locus, which is precisely why it can produce insight through cross-level reorganization.

misconceptionBranches.misconception: The goals that drive relevance realization can be specified by an external designer or programmer.
misconceptionBranches.correction: For relevance realization to be non-circular, the originating goals must be constitutive — internal to the self-organizing living system — not represented targets assigned from outside.

checkForUnderstanding.prompt: How do the constraints of auto-poetic grounding and scale-invariant self-organization together explain why relevance realization cannot be replicated by a standard computational system, and what kind of system would satisfy both constraints?

modules.title: Meaning, Relevance, and Agency: The Constitutive Triangle
modules.learningObjective: Articulate how meaning, relevance, and agency form a mutually grounding triangle and why this connection explains why the meaning crisis is also a crisis of agency and relevance.

expositionPassages.phrase: what you're now doing is you're moving from a logical to a psychological account of similarity
expositionPassages.note: The shift from formal to psychological grounds for relevance

socraticSeeds.prompt: When something feels deeply meaningful, does it also feel highly relevant? And when nothing feels relevant — when you are deeply depressed or alienated — does meaning also collapse? What does this suggest about the connection between meaning and relevance?

Agency — the sense that you are genuinely acting and making a difference — seems to require that your actions are relevant to outcomes you care about. Can you be a genuine agent in a world where nothing matters to you? What does this reveal?

Vervaeke says the connections among meaning, relevance, and agency are 'deep' — not merely correlational but constitutive. What would it mean for three things to be mutually constitutive rather than just correlated?

If meaning, relevance, and agency form a triangle that rises or falls together, what does this imply about how to address the meaning crisis? Is it enough to work on meaning directly, or must one also work on relevance and agency?

misconceptionBranches.misconception: Meaning is a feeling or belief — something purely subjective that exists independently of relevance or action.
misconceptionBranches.correction: Vervaeke argues meaning, relevance, and agency are constitutively entangled: meaning is the sense of connectedness constitutive of relevance, and agency is the relational enactment of that connection — none of the three can be adequately described in isolation.

checkForUnderstanding.prompt: In what sense does the meaning crisis represent a crisis of relevance realization — and what would it mean to address the meaning crisis at the level of relevance realization rather than at the level of new beliefs or propositions?
expectedThemes: transforming the process not the products
